mūla.devanāgarī: न च मां तानि कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनंजय | उदासीनवद् आसीनम् असक्तं तेषु कर्मसु
mūla.iast: na ca māṃ tāni karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanaṃjaya | udāsīnavad āsīnam asaktaṃ teṣu karmasu
mūla.chapter_position: Chapter 9 (Rāja-Vidyā-Rāja-Guhya-Yoga (The Yoga of Royal Knowledge and Royal Mystery)), verse 9
mūla.speaker: Krishna

senses_attested_in_panel.sense: विषमसृष्ट्यादीनि कर्माणि मां निबध्नन्ति मयि नैर्घृण्यादिकं न आपादयन्ति? यतः क्षेत्रज्ञानां पूर्वकृत्यानि
मां निबध्नन्ति मयि नैर्घृण्यादिकं न आपादयन्ति? यतः क्षेत्रज्ञानां पूर्वकृत्यानि
तानि च देवादिभावहेतूनि। अहं तु तेषु कर्मसु प्रकृतौ च उदासीनवदितिमाया परैत्यभिमुखे च विलज्जमाना इत्यादिवाक्यात्। वैषम्यन
कुर्वतस्तव जीववद्वन्धः कथं न स्यादित्याशङ्क्याह -- नचेति
कुतो न बध्नन्ति तत्राह -- उदासीनवदासीनं? यथा कश्चिदुपेक्षको द्वयोर्विवदमानयोर्जयपराजयासंसर्गी तत्कृतहर्षविषादाभ्यामसंसृ
धनंजय। तत्र कर्मणां असंबन्धित्वे कारणमाह -- उदासीनवत् आसीनं यथा उदासीनः उपेक्षकः कश्चित् तद्वत् आसीनम्? आत्मनः अविक्रिय
मयि नैर्घृण्यादिकं न आपादयन्ति? यतः क्षेत्रज्ञानां पूर्वकृत्यानि
? आत्मनः अविक्रियत्वात्? असक्तं फलासङ्गरहितम्? अभिमानवर्जितम् अहं करोमि इति तेषु कर्मसु
कर्मस्वसक्तम् इत्युक्ते सति अकर्तृत्वादिभ्रमः स्यादिति तन्निरासायोक्तंतत्र वैषम्य इति
। अतः अन्यस्यापि कर्तृत्वाभिमानाभावः फलासङ्गाभावश्च असंबन्धकारणम्? अन्यथा कर्मभिः बध्यते मूढः कोशकारवत् इत्यभिप्रायः।।तत

corpus_provenance.mūla: Belvalkar critical edition (BORI 1947), via Ambuda multi-witness

so_what_questions: If the Lord acts without being bound, does that mean action itself is inherently binding only for those who identify as doers — and if so, what exactly constitutes that identification?

Madhva insists 'udāsīnavat but not udāsīna' — what is the precise difference between resembling indifference and being indifferent, and why does that difference matter for understanding divine agency?

Rāmānuja and Vallabha both appeal to Brahma-sūtra 2.1.34-35 to defend the Lord against the charge of cruelty in unequal creation — does prior karma as a mediating cause fully dissolve the problem of theodicy, or does it only push it back a step?
Madhusūdana describes creation as mithyābhūta (of appearance-nature) — does that framing make the suffering of beings within that creation less real, and how should a practitioner hold that tension?

Śrīdhara grounds freedom from bondage in āptakāmatva (fulfilled desire) — is this a state achievable by human practitioners, or is it structurally unique to the divine, and what are the practical implications of each answer?

All six schools agree that asakti (non-attachment) is the operative cause of freedom from karma — yet they disagree on whether the Lord is a witness, a regulator, a dream-experiencer, or a līlā-player. Does the agreement on mechanism without agreement on metaphysics tell us something about what the verse is actually teaching?

If action performed without doer-identification leaves no karmic residue, can a practitioner ever truly perform action 'as the Lord does' — or does attempting to model divine detachment risk a subtle spiritual bypassing of genuine engagement?

everyday_applications.advaita: When you complete a significant project, notice the impulse to claim ownership of the outcome — 'I built this.' Advaita practice here is to observe that impulse as arising in consciousness rather than identifying with it. The work happened; let the work carry its own weight without your signature.
everyday_applications.viśiṣṭādvaita: When you observe deep inequality around you — unequal births, unequal circumstances — resist the reflex to either justify or despair. Rāmānuja's lens: each person's situation is the precise, compassionate response of Bhagavān to that person's entire prior trajectory. Your role is not cosmic explanation but loving service within whatever situation you encounter.
everyday_applications.dvaita: Before any significant decision, practice the acknowledgment that your very capacity to decide — your discernment (viveka), your energy, the circumstances aligned — exists by Hari's anugraha (grace), not by self-provision. This is not passivity; Madhva's Lord actively regulates. It is accurate accounting of where agency actually lives.
everyday_applications.śuddhādvaita: Perform your daily work as an act of Kṛṣṇa's own expression through you — not as your service to him from a distance, but as his līlā unfolding through this particular form. The freedom from bondage comes not from detachment as withdrawal but from recognizing that the actor behind your action is already free.
everyday_applications.bhakti: Audit your motivations before acting: are you acting from want (seeking something you lack) or from fullness (expressing something you already are)? Śrīdhara's āptakāmatva principle applied: the more your action arises from a sense of completeness rather than lack, the lighter the karmic residue it leaves.
everyday_applications.advaita-bhakti: When you are pulled into a conflict between two people or two factions, practice what Madhusūdana calls the udāsīna quality: be fully present and operationally engaged without letting either party's victory or defeat destabilize your inner composure. This is not indifference — you act — but action from a place of unbroken witnessing.
